title: Agent Tools
description: Learn how Doxa's documentation assistant searches and reads MDX through contentStore-backed tools.
content:
The Doxa assistant is intentionally narrow. It receives a system prompt with the docs file tree, then uses two tools to find and read documentation content.

## Tool Surface

| Tool | Purpose |
| :-- | :-- |
| `grep` | Search documentation content for a pattern and return file paths with line numbers. |
| `cat` | Read the full raw MDX content for a documentation file. |

Both tools read through `contentStore`, so they work with the bundled Vite source and the runtime GitHub adapter.

## Agent Workflow

## Data Flow

## What The Assistant Is Told

The system prompt defines strict behavior:

* Answer using only available documentation content.
* Use `grep` first when the right file is uncertain.
* Use `cat` to read full context before answering.
* Include links in the `/docs/path#section-anchor` format.
* Include code snippets when `aiConfig.codeSnippets` is enabled.
* Say when a question is outside the documentation scope.

## Tool Result Example

```text
content-sources/index.mdx:17:Two content adapters ship with Doxa: Vite and GitHub.
content-sources/index.mdx:41:Set CONTENT_SOURCE=github to fetch docs at runtime.
```

The answer should cite the relevant page instead of inventing deployment or configuration details.

## Improving Agent Answers

* Use specific h2 headings that match reader questions.
* Put key terms in frontmatter keywords.
* Keep examples complete.
* Prefer explicit configuration tables over long prose.
* Regenerate search data after content changes.

title: Chat With Docs
description: Configure and understand Doxa's built-in AI chat drawer for documentation-aware answers.
content:
Chat with Docs gives readers a side panel where they can ask questions about the documentation they are reading. The assistant is constrained to the docs corpus and uses tools to search and read MDX before answering.

## Reader Experience

The chat button appears in the navigation on `/docs` routes when `Settings.features.chatWithDocs` is enabled. The drawer supports:

* Streaming assistant responses.
* Visible tool-call activity.
* Expandable tool output.
* Conversation history stored in the browser.
* New conversation and delete controls.
* Expanded and compact drawer widths.

## Request Flow

## Enable Chat

## Provider Options

## Failure Modes

| Symptom | Likely cause | Fix |
| :-- | :-- | :-- |
| Chat returns a missing key error | `AI_API_KEY` is not set | Add the provider key to the environment. |
| Answers miss new pages | Search data is stale or docs were not deployed | Regenerate search data and redeploy. |
| The assistant cannot cite a topic | The topic is not documented | Add or expand the relevant MDX page. |
| Tool calls show no matches | Query terms do not appear in docs | Improve headings, descriptions, and keywords. |

title: llms.txt
description: Expose Doxa documentation to external AI tools through llms.txt and llms-full.txt routes.
content:
Doxa exposes AI-native text routes so external assistants and coding tools can discover, read, and cite your documentation.

## Routes

| Route | Output |
| :-- | :-- |
| `/llms.txt` | A plain-text index of all docs pages with titles, URLs, and descriptions. |
| `/llms-full.txt` | The full docs corpus concatenated into one plain-text document with source URLs. |

Both routes are generated at request time from `contentStore`, so they follow the active content adapter.

## How It Works

## Example Index Shape

```text showLineNumbers
# doxa

> Beautiful, fast documentation that stays out of your way.

## Docs

- [Overview](https://docs.example.com/docs/overview): See what Doxa Docs can do.
- [Quickstart](https://docs.example.com/docs/quickstart): Install and run Doxa.
```

## Example Full-Corpus Shape

```text showLineNumbers
# doxa

> Beautiful, fast documentation that stays out of your way.

---

## Overview
Source: https://docs.example.com/docs/overview

Doxa is a production-ready documentation template...
```

## When To Use Each Route

| Need | Use |
| :-- | :-- |
| Let an AI tool discover available pages | `/llms.txt` |
| Give an AI tool the full corpus in one fetch | `/llms-full.txt` |
| Cite a specific docs page | URLs listed in either route |
| Keep private docs private | Do not expose these routes publicly without access controls |

## Verification

_searchMeta.cleanContent: doxa exposes ai-native text routes so external assistants and coding tools can discover read and cite your documentation routes route output:-- :-- llms txt a plain-text index of all docs pages with titles urls and descriptions llms-full txt the full docs corpus concatenated into one plain-text document with source urls both routes are generated at request time from contentstore so they follow the active content adapter how it works example index shape example full-corpus shape when to use each route need use:-- :--let an ai tool discover available pages llms txtgive an ai tool the full corpus in one fetch llms-full txtcite a specific docs page urls listed in either routekeep private docs private do not expose these routes publicly without access controls verification

title: Code Blocks
description: Show commands, config files, source snippets, line numbers, line highlights, and language alternatives in Doxa docs.
content:
Code examples are one of Doxa's core reading surfaces. Fenced code blocks support language-aware highlighting, optional line numbers, line highlighting, and a copy button.

## Commands

Use `bash` for terminal commands.

```bash
pnpm install
pnpm dev
```

## Config Files

Use `showLineNumbers` on multi-line config examples.

```ts showLineNumbers
export const Settings = {
  site: {
    name: "doxa",
    url: "https://docs.example.com",
  },
  features: {
    chatWithDocs: true,
    tableOfContents: true,
  },
}
```

## Highlight Important Lines

Line highlighting helps readers find the part that matters.

```ts {6-7} showLineNumbers
export const aiConfig = {
  provider: "openrouter",
  model: "z-ai/glm-4.7:nitro",
  codeSnippets: true,
  maxContextChunks: 12,
  maxResponseTokens: 2048,
}
```

## Titles In Surrounding Text

Code titles are most reliable when you introduce the file path in prose, then show the code below.

`src/lib/components.ts`

```ts showLineNumbers
export const components = {
  Card,
  CardGrid,
  Note,
  Step,
  StepItem,
  Tabs,
  TabsContent,
  TabsList,
  TabsTrigger,
}
```

## Provider Alternatives

Tabs keep related alternatives in one place.

## Complete Examples

Avoid abbreviated code in docs that users are expected to run.

## Code Block Checklist

* Use a language identifier on every fence.
* Use `showLineNumbers` only for examples longer than a few lines.
* Highlight only the lines that matter.
* Put commands in `bash`, plain URLs in `text`, and config in `ts`, `json`, or `yaml`.
* Keep package-manager alternatives in tabs.

title: Diagrams And Math
description: Render Mermaid diagrams and KaTeX math in Doxa for architecture, workflows, relationships, and formulas.
content:
Doxa includes Mermaid for diagrams and KaTeX for mathematical notation. Use them when a visual model or equation clarifies the written explanation.

## Architecture Diagram

## Agent Sequence

## Entity Relationships

## Math Examples

Inline math is useful for small formulas like $$queryWords \ge 3$$.

Use block math for scoring and ranking explanations:

```math
relevance = \frac{title + headings + keywords + content}{\log(length + 1)}
```

```math
latencyBudget = renderTime + searchTime + modelTime
```

## Mermaid Patterns

| Diagram type | Use it for |
| :-- | :-- |
| Flowchart | Architecture, routing, deployment, and content pipelines. |
| Sequence diagram | AI calls, request lifecycles, and handoffs between systems. |
| ER diagram | Data models and relationships. |
| Decision tree | Configuration choices and troubleshooting paths. |

## Practical Rule

One good diagram can anchor a complex page. Several decorative diagrams make the page slower to read.

title: Markdown
description: Write clean documentation with GitHub-flavored Markdown, tables, checklists, links, blockquotes, images, and math.
content: Doxa supports standard Markdown through MDX, so writers can start with familiar syntax and introduce components only where they add value. ## Text And Links Use normal Markdown for paragraphs, emphasis, inline code, and links. ```md Doxa renders **bold text**, _emphasis_, `inline code`, and [links](/docs/overview). ``` That renders as: Doxa renders **bold text**, *emphasis*, `inline code`, and [links](/docs/overview). ## Lists Use unordered lists for related facts and ordered lists for sequential ideas that do not need the visual Step component. * MDX files are easy to review in pull requests. * Frontmatter powers page metadata. * Headings feed the right-side table of contents. 1. Draft the page. 2. Add examples. 3. Regenerate search data. 4. Review the rendered page. ## Checklists Checklists are useful for operational docs and release readiness. * \[x] Write the page frontmatter. * \[x] Add one concrete example. * \[ ] Verify internal links. * \[ ] Regenerate the search index. ## Tables Tables work well for settings, flags, limits, and comparisons. | Feature | Markdown syntax | When to use | | :-- | :-- | :-- | | Checklist | `- [ ] Task` | Launch or review workflows. | | Table | `| Column | Value |` | Settings and reference data. | | Blockquote | `> Text` | Quotes and short editorial emphasis. | | Code fence | \`\`\`ts | Commands, examples, and config. | ## Blockquotes > Good docs reduce the number of decisions a reader has to hold in memory. Use blockquotes sparingly. If the message is operationally important, use a `Note` instead. ## Images Images use standard Markdown syntax and can include an optional title that becomes a caption. ```md ![Doxa screenshot](/screens/screen-1.png "Doxa documentation interface") ``` ![Doxa screenshot](/screens/screen-1.png "Doxa documentation interface") ## Math Inline math works with double-dollar syntax, so you can write search or ranking examples like $$score = relevance / latency$$. Block math uses fenced `math` code blocks: ```math score = \frac{titleMatch + headingMatch + keywordMatch}{\log(contentLength + 1)} ``` ## Authoring Checklist * Use h2 and h3 headings in MDX body content. * Do not repeat the page title as an h1. * Add language identifiers to every code block. * Keep tables narrow enough to scan on smaller screens. * Use MDX components for navigation, warnings, workflows, diagrams, and file structure.

title: MDX Components
description: Use Doxa's built-in MDX components for navigation cards, callouts, steps, tabs, diagrams, and file trees.
content:
Doxa registers a focused component set for documentation pages in `src/lib/components.ts`. These components are available directly in MDX without per-page imports.

## Component Catalog

| Component | Use it for |
| :-- | :-- |
| `CardGrid` and `Card` | Navigation, feature groups, related pages, and external links. |
| `Note` | Important callouts, prerequisites, warnings, and success states. |
| `Step` and `StepItem` | Sequential workflows. |
| `Tabs` | Alternative commands, providers, platforms, or languages. |
| `Mermaid` | Architecture diagrams, agent flows, and process charts. |
| `FileTree`, `Folder`, and `File` | Project structure and content layout. |

## Cards

Use large cards for section navigation and compact cards for related links.

```jsx showLineNumbers
<CardGrid>
  <Card
    subtitle="Authoring"
    title="Code Blocks"
    description="Show commands, configuration, and source snippets."
    href="/docs/authoring/code-blocks"
  />
</CardGrid>
```

## Notes

Use notes for information a reader should not miss.

<Note title="Success" type="success">
  Use success notes to confirm expected output or completion states.
</Note>

<Note title="Danger" type="danger">
  Use danger notes for irreversible actions, security risks, or destructive commands.
</Note>

## Steps

Use `Step` for workflows that must happen in order.

## Tabs

Tabs are useful when the user chooses one option from a small set.

## File Tree

Use file trees to explain physical structure.

## Mermaid

Use Mermaid when the relationship is easier to understand visually.

## Composition Rules

* Put cards in grids; do not place standalone cards between paragraphs.
* Use steps for sequential work, not feature lists.
* Keep notes short enough to scan.
* Use tabs only when all tab values are mutually exclusive.
* Prefer one Mermaid diagram per page section.

title: Configuration
description: Configure Doxa site metadata, feature toggles, AI providers, analytics, SEO, and content sources.
content:
Doxa configuration is split by concern. Site and SEO defaults live in `src/settings/main.ts`, AI provider settings live in `src/settings/ai.ts`, and content backend selection lives in `src/settings/content.ts`.

## Site Settings

`Settings` controls public metadata, branding, analytics, and feature flags.

```ts {8-12,35-43} showLineNumbers
export const Settings = {
  site: {
    name: "doxa",
    url: "https://docs.example.com",
    description: "Beautiful, fast documentation that stays out of your way.",
    keywords: ["documentation", "mdx", "ai"],
    icon: "/favicon.svg",
  },
  features: {
    branding: true,
    rightSidebar: true,
    feedbackEdit: true,
    tableOfContents: true,
    scrollToTop: true,
    chatWithDocs: true,
    loadFromGithub: false,
  },
}
```

| Setting group | Purpose |
| :-- | :-- |
| `site` | Name, URL, description, keywords, and favicon. |
| `company` | Footer company name and link. |
| `seo` | Open Graph image, alt text, and social handle. |
| `analytics` | Google Tag Manager id and enablement flag. |
| `features` | Toggles for docs shell behavior. |
| `openGraph` / `twitter` | Social metadata used by the root document. |

## AI Provider

The docs chat uses TanStack AI. Configure the provider and model in `src/settings/ai.ts`, then set `AI_API_KEY` in the environment.

<Note title="Secret required" type="warning">
  The chat endpoint returns a configuration error when `AI_API_KEY` is missing.
  Set the key in local `.env` files and in your deployment provider.
</Note>

## Environment Variables

| Variable | Required | Used for |
| :-- | :--: | :-- |
| `VITE_SITE_URL` | Production | Canonical URLs, sitemap, Open Graph, and AI-native route URLs. |
| `AI_API_KEY` | When chat is enabled | Provider authentication for Chat with Docs. |
| `CONTENT_SOURCE` | Optional | Selects `vite` or `github`. Defaults to `vite`. |
| `DOXA_GITHUB_OWNER` | GitHub source | Repository owner for runtime docs loading. |
| `DOXA_GITHUB_REPO` | GitHub source | Repository name for runtime docs loading. |
| `DOXA_GITHUB_TOKEN` | Private GitHub docs | Read-only token for private docs repositories. |

## Feature Toggle Guidance

* Keep `chatWithDocs` enabled for demos that showcase the AI assistant.
* Disable `feedbackEdit` unless `GitHubLink.href` points at a real repository.
* Keep `tableOfContents` enabled for long reference pages.
* Use `loadFromGithub` as a product-facing toggle only after `CONTENT_SOURCE=github` is configured.

## Validate Configuration

title: Content Sources
description: Choose between bundled Vite documentation and runtime GitHub documentation with Doxa's content adapter layer.
content:
Doxa reads documentation through a single `contentStore` API. The active adapter decides where MDX comes from, while route rendering, AI tools, search-adjacent metadata, and AI-native routes keep the same interface.

## Adapter Options

| Source | Where content lives | Best for |
| :-- | :-- | :-- |
| `vite` | `src/contents/docs` in the app repo | Fast bundled docs shipped with the site. |
| `github` | A GitHub repository fetched at runtime | Content teams editing docs separately from the app. |

## Vite Source

The Vite adapter is the default. It bundles MDX with the app at build time.

```bash
CONTENT_SOURCE=vite
```

Use this when docs and site code are shipped together.

## GitHub Source

The GitHub adapter fetches MDX through the GitHub REST API and caches results.

```bash showLineNumbers
CONTENT_SOURCE=github
DOXA_GITHUB_OWNER=your-org
DOXA_GITHUB_REPO=your-docs-repo
DOXA_GITHUB_BRANCH=main
DOXA_GITHUB_BASE_PATH=src/contents/docs
DOXA_GITHUB_TOKEN=ghp_xxx
DOXA_GITHUB_CACHE_TTL=300
```

## Shared Contract

The adapters implement the same operations:

| Method | Returns |
| :-- | :-- |
| `listFiles()` | All MDX paths relative to the docs root. |
| `readFile(filePath)` | Raw MDX for a file, or `null` when missing. |
| `name` | Adapter name for logs and debugging. |

## Choosing A Source

## Operational Notes

* Search data is generated from local MDX during build-time workflows.
* Runtime GitHub content is ideal for route rendering and AI-native reads.
* Keep folder structure consistent across sources.
* Document custom adapters if you add R2, KV, Supabase, Notion, or another backend.

## GitHub Source

The GitHub adapter fetches MDX through the GitHub REST API and caches results.

bash showLineNumbers
CONTENT_SOURCE=github
DOXA_GITHUB_OWNER=your-org
DOXA_GITHUB_REPO=your-docs-repo
DOXA_GITHUB_BRANCH=main
DOXA_GITHUB_BASE_PATH=src/contents/docs
DOXA_GITHUB_TOKEN=ghp_xxx
DOXA_GITHUB_CACHE_TTL=300

## Shared Contract

The adapters implement the same operations:

| Method | Returns |
| :-- | :-- |
|

| All MDX paths relative to the docs root. |
|
| Raw MDX for a file, or

title: Customization
description: Customize Doxa branding, theme, colors, icons, MDX components, navigation, and metadata.
content:
Doxa is designed to be edited directly. Most customization happens through settings files, registered components, and static assets rather than a plugin system.

## Customization Map

| Area | File |
| :-- | :-- |
| Site name, URL, metadata, feature flags | `src/settings/main.ts` |
| Navigation and GitHub link | `src/settings/navigation.ts` |
| Theme choice and primary color | `src/contents/settings/theme.json` |
| Logo assets | `public/doxa-logo-*.svg`, `public/logo.svg`, `public/favicon.svg` |
| Icon aliases for MDX cards | `src/settings/icons.ts` |
| MDX component registry | `src/lib/components.ts` |
| Component usage guidance | `agent/component-playbook.md` |

## Branding

Update the site name and metadata in `Settings`.

```ts {3-7} showLineNumbers
export const Settings = {
  site: {
    name: "Acme Docs",
    url: "https://docs.acme.com",
    description: "Technical documentation for Acme developers.",
    keywords: ["acme", "api", "developer docs"],
    icon: "/favicon.svg",
  },
}
```

## Theme And Color

The root route loads `theme.json`, resolves the selected theme, and injects CSS variables for the active font and primary color.

```json showLineNumbers
{
  "activeTheme": "default",
  "primaryColor": "zinc"
}
```

## Icons

Cards accept icon keys from `src/settings/icons.ts`.

```ts showLineNumbers
export const iconMap = {
  alignJustify: LuAlignJustify,
  arrowUpRight: LuArrowUpRight,
}
```

Then use the key in MDX:

```jsx
<Card
  title="Navigation"
  href="/docs/navigation"
  icon="alignJustify"
  variant="small"
/>
```

## Register MDX Components

Add MDX-available components to `src/lib/components.ts`.

```ts {8-9} showLineNumbers
export const components = {
  a: RoutedLink,
  Card,
  CardGrid,
  FileTree,
  Mermaid,
  Note,
  Step,
  StepItem,
}
```

## Customization Workflow

title: Deployment
description: Deploy Doxa Docs to Cloudflare Workers or Vercel with the right build target, environment variables, and validation steps.
content:
Doxa includes scripts for Cloudflare Workers and Vercel. Cloudflare is the default target, and Vercel has dedicated build and deploy commands.

## Deployment Targets

## Required Production Settings

| Setting | Why it matters |
| :-- | :-- |
| `VITE_SITE_URL` | Canonical tags, sitemap, robots, Open Graph URLs, and `llms.txt` source links. |
| `AI_API_KEY` | Required when Chat with Docs is enabled. |
| `CONTENT_SOURCE` | Selects bundled or runtime docs content. |
| GitHub adapter vars | Required only when `CONTENT_SOURCE=github`. |

## Cloudflare Workers

## Vercel

## Preflight Checklist

* \[x] `pnpm generate-content-json` has been run after content changes.
* \[x] `src/contents/settings/documents.json` has no stale demo routes.
* \[x] `VITE_SITE_URL` points at the production docs URL.
* \[x] `AI_API_KEY` is set when chat is enabled.
* \[x] `/llms.txt` and `/llms-full.txt` return the expected content.
* \[x] Search opens with `Command+K` or `Ctrl+K`.

title: Navigation
description: Control sidebar order, page headings, spacers, and generated navigation metadata in Doxa.
content:
Doxa separates page discovery from navigation design. MDX files live in folders, while `src/contents/settings/documents.json` controls the sidebar order, section headings, spacers, and page titles shown in the docs shell.

## Navigation Model

Each route entry maps to a folder under `src/contents/docs`. The app prefixes these hrefs with `/docs` at render time.

```json showLineNumbers
[
  { "heading": "Start Here" },
  { "title": "Overview", "href": "/overview" },
  { "title": "Quickstart", "href": "/quickstart" },
  { "spacer": true },
  { "heading": "Authoring" },
  { "title": "Markdown", "href": "/authoring/markdown" }
]
```

| Entry | Purpose |
| :-- | :-- |
| `heading` | Adds a non-clickable section label to the sidebar. |
| `title` + `href` | Adds a clickable docs page. |
| `spacer` | Adds visual separation between groups. |
| `noLink` | Keeps a page in the route data without rendering it as a link. |

## Folder Structure

The physical folder names become the URL path. A page at `src/contents/docs/ai/chat-with-docs/index.mdx` resolves to `/docs/ai/chat-with-docs`.

## Adding A Page

## Generator Behavior

`pnpm generate:docs` discovers new MDX pages and appends missing entries to `documents.json`.

## Navigation Checklist

* \[x] Every visible page has a route entry.
* \[x] The first route is the page linked by the home page "Get Started" button.
* \[x] Section headings describe user intent, not file layout.
* \[x] Stale demo pages are removed from `documents.json`.
* \[x] Search data is regenerated after large content changes.

title: Overview
description: See how Doxa Docs handles rich MDX authoring, AI chat, fast search, flexible content sources, and edge-ready deployment.
content:
Doxa is a production-ready documentation template for teams that want the docs site, authoring system, and AI experience to feel like one product. It pairs TanStack Start, React 19, Tailwind CSS v4, TypeScript, MDX, and TanStack AI with a focused set of documentation primitives.

## What Doxa Ships

| Capability | What it gives you |
| :-- | :-- |
| **MDX documentation** | Markdown plus React components registered in `src/lib/components.ts`. |
| **AI chat** | A streaming chat drawer that searches and reads the docs before answering. |
| **Search** | Fuzzy search backed by generated page metadata, headings, snippets, and keywords. |
| **AI-native routes** | `/llms.txt` and `/llms-full.txt` for external AI tools and code assistants. |
| **Content adapters** | Bundle docs with Vite or fetch them from GitHub at runtime. |
| **Edge deployment** | Cloudflare Workers by default, with Vercel support included. |
| **Customization** | Theme, color, logo, metadata, icons, navigation, and MDX registry settings. |

## System Flow

The important detail is that Doxa's rendering, search, AI agent, and AI-native exports all read from the same content access layer. Change the content source once and the rest of the app keeps the same contract.

## Best Fit

Doxa is a good fit for:

* Product documentation with code-heavy guides.
* API or SDK docs that need great code blocks and predictable navigation.
* Internal engineering handbooks that benefit from fast search.
* AI-ready knowledge bases where `/llms.txt` and chat are first-class.
* Teams that want content in GitHub but still need a polished reading experience.

## Where To Go Next

title: Project Structure
description: Understand the Doxa folder layout, content pipeline, MDX registry, and generated assets.
content:
Doxa keeps the authoring surface small: MDX content goes in one tree, reusable components live in predictable folders, and generated data is committed under `public/search-data`.

## Important Paths

## How Content Moves

## Source Of Truth

| Area | Source file | Notes |
| :-- | :-- | :-- |
| Page content | `src/contents/docs/**/index.mdx` | Markdown and MDX examples. |
| Sidebar order | `src/contents/settings/documents.json` | Curated manually for order and headings. |
| MDX components | `src/lib/components.ts` | Registers what MDX can render. |
| Search metadata | `public/search-data/documents.json` | Generated from MDX content. |
| AI behavior | `src/lib/agent/system-prompt.ts` | Constrains the assistant to documentation content. |
| Content backend | `src/settings/content.ts` | Selects Vite or GitHub content. |

## Naming Rules

* Use folder names for stable URL slugs.
* Use frontmatter titles for human-readable page names.
* Keep example pages focused on one job.
* Prefer section names based on user intent: "AI & Search" is clearer than "Internals".

title: Quickstart
description: Install Doxa Docs, run the development server, edit your first MDX page, and verify the generated search data.
content:
Use this guide to get from a fresh clone to a running Doxa site with editable content and working search metadata.

## Install And Run

## Edit Your First Page

Documentation pages live under `src/contents/docs`. Every page is an `index.mdx` file inside a folder that becomes the URL slug.

```markdown showLineNumbers
---
title: First Guide
description: A first guide written in Doxa.
keywords: ["guide", "mdx", "doxa"]
---

This page supports **Markdown**, `inline code`, and MDX components.

<Note title="Success" type="success">
  You are writing Doxa documentation.
</Note>
```

## Refresh Search Data

Search, table of contents, and page metadata are derived from `public/search-data/documents.json`. Regenerate it after meaningful docs changes.

```bash
pnpm generate-content-json
```

## Common Commands

| Command | Use |
| :-- | :-- |
| `pnpm dev` | Start local development using the default Cloudflare target. |
| `pnpm build` | Create a production build using the default target. |
| `pnpm start` | Preview a production build locally. |
| `pnpm generate-content-json` | Rebuild the search and metadata JSON. |
| `pnpm generate:docs` | Append newly discovered MDX pages to `documents.json`. |
| `pnpm lint` | Run ESLint. |

## Next Steps

title: Search
description: Understand Doxa's generated search index, fuzzy matching, highlighted snippets, and keyboard search flow.
content:
Doxa includes a search modal for fast page discovery. It reads generated metadata from `public/search-data/documents.json`, ranks results client-side, and highlights matching terms in snippets.

## Search Experience

* Press `Command+K` on macOS or `Ctrl+K` on Windows and Linux.
* Type at least three characters.
* Press `Enter` to open the first result.
* Click any result to navigate to that page.

## Index Pipeline

## What Gets Indexed

| Source | Search use |
| :-- | :-- |
| Frontmatter `title` | High-weight result title. |
| Frontmatter `description` | Result context and metadata. |
| Frontmatter `keywords` | High-weight matching terms. |
| h2 headings | Table of contents and relevance signals. |
| Body content | Snippet extraction and general matching. |
| Inline code and bold text | Additional extracted keywords. |

## Regenerate Search Data

```bash
pnpm generate-content-json
```

The script parses MDX, removes component wrappers from indexed prose, extracts headings and keywords, and writes the combined JSON file.

## Ranking Model

Doxa combines title, heading, keyword, exact content, and proximity signals. A simplified form looks like this:

```math
score = \frac{title + headings + keywords + exactMatches + proximity}{\log(contentLength + 1)}
```

## Content Tips

* Use reader-language titles such as "Chat With Docs" instead of internal names like "Agent UI".
* Put synonyms in `keywords` when a feature has multiple names.
* Keep descriptions specific because they appear in search and metadata.
* Use h2 headings for major topics users may search for.

## Search Troubleshooting

| Problem | Fix |
| :-- | :-- |
| New page does not appear | Add it to `documents.json` and regenerate search data. |
| Result title is wrong | Fix the page frontmatter title. |
| Search finds stale content | Run `pnpm generate-content-json`. |
| Relevant page ranks poorly | Improve headings, keywords, and repeated topic language. |
